core_mechanism: Alignment of an embedded body's principal axes and angular-momentum vector with the eigenvector directions of an external anisotropic deformation (tidal) field, where the angular momentum is fixed impulsively by the single dominant early perturbation and retains that orientation as a memory while later impulses reorient only the outer layers
brief_reason: The abstract reports preferential alignment of the halos' minor axes and disk angular momentum with the sheet normal / direction of maximum collapse, with L_disk set by the largest early merger and retaining its imprint while only recent mergers twist the outer shape.
